zh.lifeSummary: 當Charles Henry Tilley於1413年出生在英國時，他的父親William Tilley19歲，母親Mrs William Tilley29歲。他於大約1435年與Agnes Johnstone Alexander在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1469年8月12日在英國英格蘭貝德福爾特拉爾 貝德福爾享年56歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭貝德福爾特拉爾 貝德福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Tilley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Tilly-sur-Seulles (Calvados) or Tilly (Eure), France.

English: habitational name from Tyley in Wotton under Edge (Gloucestershire), Tiley in Minterne Magna (Dorset), or Tilley (Shropshire). The Gloucestershire and Dorset placenames derive from Old English tigel ‘tile’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. The Shropshire placename derives from an uncertain first element + Old English lēah.
English: occupational name from Middle English tilie ‘one who tills the land, a husbandman’. Compare Tiller .

variants.name: Tillie
meanings: English (northern England and southern Scotland): variant of   Tilley  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Tiller
meanings: English (southern, especially Hampshire): occupational name from Middle English tiliere, tilyer ‘one who tills the soil, husbandman, farmer, farm laborer’. This is a derivative of Old English tilian ‘to till (the soil)’, Middle English tilie. There has been some confusion of this name with Tyler .

variants.name: Tiley
meanings: English (Gloucestershire, Somerset and Wiltshire): variant of   Tilley  .

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Charles Henry Tilley was born in 1413, in England, his father, William Tilley, was 19 and his mother, Mrs William Tilley, was 29. He married Agnes Johnstone Alexander about 1435, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 12 August 1469, in Central Bedfordshire, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56, and was buried in Central Bedfordshire, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Charles Henry Tilley(이)가 1413년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Tilley(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs William Tilley(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1435년 영국에서 Agnes Johnstone Alexander(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1469년 8월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 세트럴 베드퍼드실르에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 세트럴 베드퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Charles Henry Tilley родился в 1413 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени William Tilley было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs William Tilley, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Johnstone Alexander около 1435 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 12 августа 1469 г. в местности кентраль бедфордшир, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности кентраль бедфордшир, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
